ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Sabinianus, Deacon.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: In the cause of our brother the most reverend John, bishop of Constantinople, I have been unwilling to write two letters.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But one I have drawn up briefly, which may seem to combine both requisites; that is to say, both honesty and kindness.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Let therefore thy Love take care to give him this letter which I have now addressed to him in compliance with the wish of the Emperor.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For in the sequel another will be sent him such as his pride will not rejoice in.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For he has come even to this; that, taking occasion of the case of John the presbyter, he transmitted hither the acts, wherein almost in every line he called himself οἰκουμενικὸν (œcumenical) patriarch.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But I hope in Almighty God that the Supernal Majesty will confound his hypocrisy.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But I wonder how he could so deceive thy Love as that thou shouldest allow the lord Emperor to be persuaded to write to me himself concerning this matter, admonishing me to have peace with him.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For, if the lord Emperor wishes to observe justice, he ought to have admonished him to refrain from the proud title, and then at once there would be peace between us.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I suspect, however, that thou hast not all considered with what cunningness this has been done by our aforesaid brother John.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it is for this purpose that he has done it; that the lord Emperor might be obeyed, and so he himself might seem to be confirmed in his vanity, or that I might not obey him, and so his mind might be irritated against me.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But we will keep to the right way, fearing nothing in this cause except the Almighty Lord.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore let thy Love be in nothing afraid.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: All things that you see to be lofty in this world against the truth in behalf of the truth despise; trust in the grace of Almighty God, and the help of the blessed Apostle Peter.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Remember the voice of the Truth, which says, Greater is he that is in you than he that is in the world (1 John iv.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: 4); and in this cause whatever has to be done, do it with the utmost authority.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For now that we can in no wise be protected from the swords of our enemies, now that for love of the republic we have lost silver, gold, slaves and clothing, it is too ignominious that through those men we should lose even the faith.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For to assent to that atrocious title is nothing else than to lose the faith.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore, as I have written to thee already in former letters, never do thou presume to proceed with him.
